summary.intent: Audit the completed overlay agnostic epic (#222) for spec/implementation compliance, then split the two bloated overlay test aggregators (spec_conformance/overlay.rs and proptest_state_machines/overlay.rs) into per-component directory-backed submodules.
summary.outcome: Both test aggregators were split into per-component modules under directory-backed mod.rs trees, missing spec-conformance coverage was added for dialog/popover/presence/tooltip, two dead variable suppressions introduced by the split were cleaned up post-review, all overlay tests passed at 1000 proptest cases, and the change was committed with a co-author trailer.

repo: cargo xtest is stage-oriented (unit/integration/adapter), not a focused-test wrapper; raw cargo test is still needed for filtered overlay or ignored proptest invocations with PROPTEST_CASES
Directory-backed modules use mod.rs convention; the test harnesses wire submodules via #[path = ...] attributes in spec_conformance.rs and proptest_state_machines.rs
Toast is split into two sub-modules: toast::single and toast::manager, each with their own Part enum and proptest module
cargo xtask provides spec validate, spec compile-snippets, spec info <component>, spec digest <component>, and lint snapshot-count as first-class audit commands
cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject is used for snapshot hygiene but has pre-existing failures on utility/highlight snapshots unrelated to overlay
cargo mutants requires -j N for parallel execution; serial runs are too slow for files with 90+ mutants
The project uses cargo xci-fast as a local CI gate (fmt, check, clippy, nextest unit, adapter stages) and cargo xci for the full suite

code.finding: After splitting, submodule names (dialog, popover, etc.) shadowed the ars_components::overlay::* imports. Required aliasing component imports with core_ prefix (e.g., core_dialog, core_popover) in the shared mod.rs to disambiguate type paths across all child modules.

Mechanical split left a dead let _ = before_context; — the parameter was cloned, passed into the assertion helper, and immediately ignored with no test value. Removed the parameter and call-site argument.
Same pattern as popover: let _ = before_state; was a split artifact with no behavioral value. Removed parameter and call-site capture.

Tooltip hidden-description ID uses a content-description token, not a plain hidden-description token; conformance check must account for the registered ID form rather than asserting a bare string match.

cargo xci-fast applied formatter changes to this file as a side effect of running cargo fmt --workspace; must be staged explicitly or the gate produces a dirty-tree diff unrelated to the target change.

workflow: Run focused baseline tests before any file movement to establish a clean pass/fail baseline that separates pre-existing failures from refactor regressions
Python one-shot extraction scripts are fast for mechanical splits but imprecise on brace boundaries; expect 2-3 patch rounds to fix closure/bracket mismatches after the initial extraction

target/debug/deps and target/tests/trybuild can accumulate read-only .rmeta files from prior tool runs; chmod -R u+w target/debug/deps target/tests/trybuild is a recurring fix needed before cargo xci-fast or nextest will pass
cargo mutants --list is fast and useful for sizing mutation work before committing to a long run; floating_panel has 481 mutants and dialog/tour are similarly large
For mutation testing on large files, -j 6 reduces wall time significantly but the full 11-file overlay suite is still multi-hour and needs a separate dedicated session or CI job

friction: Worktree was behind origin/main by 2 commits (menu #672, tour #673) during the planning phase; user had to manually rebase before implementation could start
Serial cargo mutants run for alert_dialog (92 mutants) took 10+ minutes; had to kill and restart with -j 6, losing intermediate results
target/debug/deps and target/tests/trybuild had read-only .rmeta permissions from prior runs, causing clippy and trybuild test failures that required chmod -R u+w to unblock

Python extraction script for the 2603-line proptest aggregator produced imprecise brace boundaries, requiring 4-5 manual apply_patch rounds to fix unclosed proptest! blocks and misplaced closing braces
Module name shadowing after split (test module names colliding with component imports) was a non-obvious failure mode that produced confusing type resolution errors before the core_ aliasing fix
cargo fmt -p ars-components --test spec_conformance --test proptest_state_machines was called with invalid flags initially; correct invocation is just cargo fmt -p ars-components
cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject fails on pre-existing utility/highlight snapshots, blocking use of that gate as a clean pass/fail signal for overlay-only changes
open_items: Mutation testing for 10 remaining overlay components (dialog, drawer, floating_panel, hover_card, popover, presence, toast/single, toast/manager, tooltip, tour) was only sized with --list; full runs with -j 6 are needed to identify and kill surviving mutants
floating_panel has 481 mutants — the largest overlay target by far — and will need dedicated mutation session time or a CI job rather than an interactive run
Pre-existing cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject failure on utility/highlight snapshots needs a separate cleanup pass before that gate can be used reliably in CI

Spec/implementation drift audit (Plan Step 5) was partially covered by the Part enum comparison script but deeper surface checks (ARIA, focus, z-index, positioning, toast semantics, tour step semantics) were not fully exercised due to time constraints from mutation testing
cargo llvm-cov coverage report was generated but branch-level uncovered overlay paths were not acted on; a coverage triage pass could surface test gaps not caught by mutation testing
